chunks.title: GEYA About and Mission
chunks.text: GEYA stands for Glenmoore Eagle Youth Association. GEYA is a nonprofit, volunteer organization. GEYA provides Recreational Soccer, HIGH 5 Soccer, Little League Baseball, and Junior American Legion Baseball programs for children in Kindergarten through High School during all seasons of the year. GEYA's mission is to pursue excellence in athletics while making teamwork, good sportsmanship, and friendship the goal of all actions. GEYA uses the collective talents of its members to promote confidence, goodwill, and leadership.

chunks.title: GEYA Junior American Legion Baseball
chunks.text: GEYA has a Junior American Legion Baseball program. The American Legion Baseball page lists a 2024 registration link and TeamSnap form for Junior American Legion Baseball. Pennsylvania American Legion Baseball includes players ages 13-19. Pennsylvania American Legion Baseball uses wood bats only. American Legion Baseball aims to prepare players for future success in baseball and in life. The program emphasizes teamwork, team loyalty, discipline, leadership, and good sportsmanship. American Legion Code of Sportsmanship: - Keep the rules. - Keep faith with teammates. - Keep temper. - Keep myself fit. - Keep a stout heart in defeat. - Keep pride under in victory. - Keep a sound soul, clean mind, and healthy body.

chunks.title: GEYA HIGH 5 Soccer
chunks.text: HIGH 5 Soccer is GEYA's fun soccer program for players with special needs. Once a week, players pair with a teen or adult buddy for soccer-based activities and games adapted to their abilities. HIGH 5 focuses on teamwork, self-esteem, sportsmanship, respect, and fun. HIGH 5 players can be ages 5 to 18. Buddies are usually in middle school or high school. Younger children who want to volunteer as buddies should contact the HIGH 5 coaches at HIGH5@geyasoccer.org. Buddies over 18 must complete the background checks required by Pennsylvania. HIGH 5 sessions are on Sundays, September 13 to November 8. All HIGH 5 sessions take place at Fellowship Field #1, 240 Fellowship Road, Chester Springs. Buddy training for new and returning buddies is Wednesday, August 26 at 7:00 PM at The Barn at Upland Farm, 301 Pottstown Pike, Chester Springs. Entrance and parking are on Darrell Drive. HIGH 5 players should bring a water bottle and wear sneakers and comfortable clothes. Players receive a HIGH 5 t-shirt on the first day

chunks.title: GEYA HIGH 5 Soccer
chunks.text: Pottstown Pike, Chester Springs. Entrance and parking are on Darrell Drive. HIGH 5 players should bring a water bottle and wear sneakers and comfortable clothes. Players receive a HIGH 5 t-shirt on the first day and should wear it to every session. Cleats and shin guards are optional. GEYA provides juice and a snack after the session. HIGH 5 has no cost. It is free to all participants thanks to donations and GEYA Soccer. HIGH 5 families can expect a fun and meaningful experience, adapted games and activities, a safe and healthy environment, and opportunities to network with other families. HIGH 5 parents need to provide transportation to and from all sessions, stay on the sidelines during sessions, and lend support in a positive manner. If a player is new to HIGH 5, Coach Matt will call before the season starts to introduce himself and answer questions. A player is a participant with special needs. A buddy is a teenager or adult who works one-on-one with a player during HIGH

chunks.title: GEYA HIGH 5 Soccer
chunks.text: will call before the season starts to introduce himself and answer questions. A player is a participant with special needs. A buddy is a teenager or adult who works one-on-one with a player during HIGH 5 sessions. HIGH 5 does not have a residency requirement. Children can participate no matter where they live. People can help HIGH 5 as a buddy, an activity leader, or a snack helper. Volunteers do not need soccer experience as long as they are enthusiastic, patient, and love helping kids smile. Donations can be mailed by check to GEYA Soccer, P.O. Box 164, Uwchland PA 19480, with "HIGH 5" in the notes section, or brought to a session. HIGH 5 Soccer began in September 2012 with 7 players and 12 buddies. HIGH 5 is based on US Youth Soccer's TOPSoccer model.

chunks.title: GEYA Little League Baseball
chunks.text: GEYA Little League Baseball registration for the upcoming season is handled through the GEYA Baseball main site. A February 23, 2026 GEYA post also says to register for the upcoming baseball season through that baseball main site. GEYA Little League Baseball divisions: - Tee Ball. - Minor - Coach/Machine Pitch. - Minor - Player Pitch. - Major. - Intermediate. - Junior. - Senior. Tee Ball is the entry-level division for players ages 4-7. Tee Ball is grounded in fun, fitness, and fundamentals. Tee Ball players use bats and balls designated specifically for the division. Tee Ball roster size averages 7-10 players. The season usually includes 1-2 practices per week and one weekend game. Each Little Leaguer is required to play one season of Tee Ball before being eligible for evaluation by local league officials for consideration to advance to a higher Little League baseball or softball division. Minor Coach/Machine Pitch is intended for League Age 5-8 players for a full season of Coach/Machine Pitch baseball. It is primarily for League

chunks.title: GEYA Little League Baseball
chunks.text: consideration to advance to a higher Little League baseball or softball division. Minor Coach/Machine Pitch is intended for League Age 5-8 players for a full season of Coach/Machine Pitch baseball. It is primarily for League Age 7 players with some advanced 5- and 6-year-olds and young or new 8-year-olds. League Age 5- and 6-year-olds who have completed one year of Tee Ball can participate in Minor Coach/Machine Pitch. Minor Player Pitch is for ages 8-10. Minor League Baseball programs may be operated for younger players with less experience. The minor league may include players ages 5-11, and divisions can include machine pitch, coach pitch, or player pitch. Rosters may have 12-20 players, with most rosters in the 12-15 range. The diamond is 60 feet, and the pitching distance is 46 feet. Major, also called Little League Baseball Major Division, is for boys and girls ages 9-12. A local league may choose to limit Major Division to 10-, 11-, and 12-year-olds or to 11-12-year-olds. The diamond is 60 feet, and the

chunks.title: GEYA Little League Baseball
chunks.text: League Baseball Major Division, is for boys and girls ages 9-12. A local league may choose to limit Major Division to 10-, 11-, and 12-year-olds or to 11-12-year-olds. The diamond is 60 feet, and the pitching distance is 46 feet. Intermediate 50/70 is for players league-age 11-13. It uses a 50-foot pitching distance and 70-foot base paths. It is a transition between the standard Little League field size and the Junior/Senior field size. Junior is for ages 12-14 and uses a 60/90 full-size field. Senior is for ages 13-16 and uses a 60/90 full-size field. Parents are strongly encouraged to volunteer in Little League. After completing a Little League volunteer application and passing a required national background check, parents may help with practices and may be eligible as coaches, managers, umpires, local league board members, and other volunteer positions. GEYA Baseball sponsorship opportunities are available by emailing geyasponsorships@gmail.com.

chunks.title: GEYA Registration
chunks.text: GEYA registration is handled online through program-specific registration links. Little League Baseball registration is handled through the GEYA Baseball main site. Spring Soccer registration for 2026 was posted on February 21, 2026. The Spring Soccer registration deadline is March 7, 2026. 2026 Spring Soccer registration dates: - Early Bird Registration: December 15 to January 31. - Regular Registration: February 1 to February 28. - Late Registration: March 1 to March 7. 2026 Fall Soccer registration dates: - Early Bird Registration: June 1 to June 30. - Regular Registration: July 1 to July 31. - Late Registration: August 1 to August 15. HIGH 5 Soccer player and buddy registration is available through TeamSnap. Junior American Legion Baseball has a 2024 TeamSnap registration form listed in the current indexed GEYA resources.

chunks.title: GEYA Recreational Soccer
chunks.text: GEYA Soccer is an all-volunteer recreational soccer program. GEYA Soccer divisions: - Boys: Kindergarten, 1st Grade, 2nd/3rd Grade, 4th/5th Grade, and 6th-8th Grade. - Girls: Kindergarten, 1st Grade, 2nd/3rd Grade, 4th/5th Grade, and 6th-8th Grade. - Coed: 9th-12th Grade. - HIGH 5 Soccer: players with special needs. GEYA Soccer offers a spring season, a fall season, and winter indoor futsal. Teams are coached by volunteer coaches, most of whom are parents of players on the team. 2026 Fall Soccer key dates: - Early Bird Registration: June 1 to June 30. - Regular Registration: July 1 to July 31. - Late Registration: August 1 to August 15. - Team Assignments and Coaches Meeting: week of August 31. - First practices: week of September 8, Tuesday through Thursday. - First games: Coed on September 12; K-8 on September 19. - K-3 Parents vs Players Finale: Saturday, November 7. - 4th grade through 12th grade tournament: Saturday/Sunday, November 7 and 8. 2026 Spring Soccer key dates: - Register by March 7, 2026. -

chunks.title: GEYA Recreational Soccer
chunks.text: September 19. - K-3 Parents vs Players Finale: Saturday, November 7. - 4th grade through 12th grade tournament: Saturday/Sunday, November 7 and 8. 2026 Spring Soccer key dates: - Register by March 7, 2026. - Early Bird Registration: December 15 to January 31. - Regular Registration: February 1 to February 28. - Late Registration: March 1 to March 7. - Team Assignments and Coaches Meeting: week of March 16. - First practices: March 21. - First games: Coed on March 23; everyone else on April 6. - K-3 Parents vs Players Finale: Saturday, June 6. - 4th grade through 12th grade tournament: Saturday/Sunday/Monday, May 30 to June 1. 2026 Spring Soccer fee schedule: - Early Bird: $105. - Regular: $115. - Late: $130. - Sibling discount: $15 per additional sibling after the first. - Volunteer discount for coaches and board members: $15 per season, credited manually at midseason. GEYA Soccer registration questions should be sent to registrations@geyasoccer.org or geyareg@gmail.com. General GEYA Soccer questions should go to the division commissioners. Team

chunks.title: GEYA Recreational Soccer
chunks.text: discount for coaches and board members: $15 per season, credited manually at midseason. GEYA Soccer registration questions should be sent to registrations@geyasoccer.org or geyareg@gmail.com. General GEYA Soccer questions should go to the division commissioners. Team questions should go to the team coach first. Families interested in becoming a Division Commissioner, Volunteer Coach, or Soccer Board member can contact Jay Janczak at president@geyasoccer.org. GEYA Soccer uses TeamSnap for registration and team management. Every parent should download TeamSnap, create an individual account, and then use the current season registration link. GEYA provides separate TeamSnap links for Kindergarten through 12th Grade divisions, volunteer coaches, and HIGH 5 Soccer. After registration ends, GEYA Soccer needs about 10 days to build rosters and assign coaches. Team rosters and schedules are then published, and families are notified of team assignments through TeamSnap. Coaches send first-practice information through TeamSnap. All divisions practice twice before the first game except Coed, which practices once. Schedules are posted to TeamSnap once rosters are built, roughly 10 days before first practices.

chunks.title: GEYA Recreational Soccer
chunks.text: TeamSnap. Coaches send first-practice information through TeamSnap. All divisions practice twice before the first game except Coed, which practices once. Schedules are posted to TeamSnap once rosters are built, roughly 10 days before first practices. Each soccer team plays or practices twice each week. One event is on a weeknight from Monday through Thursday, and one event is on Saturday morning or early afternoon. The weeknight event changes from week to week. GEYA Soccer equipment and player expectations: - Each player should bring a properly inflated soccer ball with their name on it. - Ball size 3 is for Kindergarten and 1st Grade. - Ball size 4 is for 2nd/3rd Grade and 4th/5th Grade. - Ball size 5 is for 6th-8th Grade and Coed. - Each player must wear shin guards under socks for all practices and games. - Cleats are recommended. - Individual water bottles are recommended, and sharing water bottles should be limited. - Earrings are not permitted. Coaches will ask players to remove them before participating. If

chunks.title: GEYA Recreational Soccer
chunks.text: practices and games. - Cleats are recommended. - Individual water bottles are recommended, and sharing water bottles should be limited. - Earrings are not permitted. Coaches will ask players to remove them before participating. If they resist, they cannot play. GEYA Soccer field locations: - Kindergarten, 1st Grade, and 2nd/3rd Grade play at Walnut Lane Park, 803 Walnut Lane, Chester Springs. There are four micro fields, and the field number is on one of the goals for each field. - 4th/5th Grade plays at Larkins Field on the corner of Byers Road and Graphite Mine Road, Chester Springs. - 6th-8th Grade plays at Fellowship Field, 241 Fellowship Road, Chester Springs. Fields 3 and 4 are upper fields, so use the upper parking lot. Field 1 is turf, so use the lower parking lot. - Coed 9th-12th Grade plays at Fellowship Field, 241 Fellowship Road, Chester Springs. Field 1 is turf, so use the lower parking lot. Fields 3 and 4 are upper fields, so use the upper parking lot. Weather

chunks.title: GEYA Recreational Soccer
chunks.text: 9th-12th Grade plays at Fellowship Field, 241 Fellowship Road, Chester Springs. Field 1 is turf, so use the lower parking lot. Fields 3 and 4 are upper fields, so use the upper parking lot. Weather decisions are typically made 90 minutes before an event starts. If families do not receive league communication by email or text, the event is still on. GEYA Soccer game conduct: - 4th-12th Grade divisions have paid referees who observe normal soccer rules for throw-ins and goal kicks. - For Kindergarten and 1st Grade, coaches enforce field boundaries reasonably during the first month, then more strictly as the season continues. - For 2nd and 3rd Grade, coaches enforce boundaries strictly from the start. - Kindergarten and 1st Grade use kick-ins. 2nd and 3rd Grade use throw-ins. - For K-5 goal kicks, the defensive team must retreat to the buildout line and allow one pass before pressuring the ball. - Players should receive at least one substitution per quarter or half. - All players should play at

chunks.title: GEYA Recreational Soccer
chunks.text: the defensive team must retreat to the buildout line and allow one pass before pressuring the ball. - Players should receive at least one substitution per quarter or half. - All players should play at least 50 percent of the game and have the opportunity to play multiple positions. - Coaches can adapt the number of players on the field sensibly according to attendance.

Your cheers, encouragement, and community spirit powered our team's unforgettable journey to the Little League World Series! Congratulations to Team Mid-Atlantic on an extraordinary season. Your grit, discipline, and relentless drive have set a new standard for excellence. Be A Part of the Action! Stay connected for game highlights, team updates, and behind-the-scenes fun from our GEYA Little League Baseball community! Division Information Tee Ball Minor - Coach/Machine Pitch Minor - Player Pitch Major Intermediate Junior Senior Tee Ball - Little League Baseball Often, a family’s first introduction to Little League® is in the Tee Ball program. Tee Ball is the entry level division for players,

chunks.title: Little League Baseball – GEYA Glenmoore Eagle Youth Association
chunks.text: Player Pitch Major Intermediate Junior Senior Tee Ball - Little League Baseball Often, a family’s first introduction to Little League® is in the Tee Ball program. Tee Ball is the entry level division for players, ages 4-7. The experience is grounded in fun, fitness, and fundamentals while also being a key to growing a child’s love for the game. The Little League Tee Ball program provides volunteer coaches with everything they need to create an environment where players will learn the skills of the game, while having fun and staying active. Parents and legal guardians of Tee Ball players are crucial to the extension of the program, and their satisfaction is equally important to its success. The outreach and inclusion of this division sets the foundation for continued participation in the local Little League program. Each Little Leaguer is required to play one season of Tee Ball before being eligible to be evaluated by the local league officials for consideration to advance to a higher division of Little League baseball

chunks.title: Little League Baseball – GEYA Glenmoore Eagle Youth Association
chunks.text: Each Little Leaguer is required to play one season of Tee Ball before being eligible to be evaluated by the local league officials for consideration to advance to a higher division of Little League baseball or softball. The Tee Ball Division is co-ed, but may be structured as a baseball or softball program. Such a decision is made by the local league’s Board of Directors.The use of a robust 10-week Tee Ball program benefits the players and volunteer coaches, who typically are younger parents with little experience as coaches/managers. Tee Ball players use bats and balls (baseballs and softballs) designated specifically for use in this division. The roster size for a Tee Ball team averages 7-10 players, with a season featuring 1-2 practices per week with a single game played on weekends. Parents are strongly encouraged to become involved in this entry level of Little League. After completing a Little League volunteer application and passing a required national background check, parents may become involved in practices, and be eligible as

chunks.title: Little League Baseball – GEYA Glenmoore Eagle Youth Association
chunks.text: encouraged to become involved in this entry level of Little League. After completing a Little League volunteer application and passing a required national background check, parents may become involved in practices, and be eligible as coaches, managers, umpires, local league board members and other volunteer positions within the league. Minor - Coach/Machine Pitch - Little League Baseball Intended for League Ages 5-8 players for a complete season of Coach/Machine Pitch baseball. Primarily League Age 7 players with some advanced 5s/6s and young/new 8s. Note: League Age 5 and 6 year olds that have completed 1 year of tee ball can participate in Minor Coach/Machine Pitch Division. Minor League Minor League Baseball programs for boys and girls may be operated within each division for younger players with less experience. The minor league may be players ages 5-11.Divisions may be established within the Minor League for “machine pitch,” “coach-pitch” or “player-pitch.” Rosters may be composed of between 12 and 20 players, but most leagues have rosters in the 12-15 range. The diamond

chunks.title: Little League Baseball – GEYA Glenmoore Eagle Youth Association
chunks.text: 5-11.Divisions may be established within the Minor League for “machine pitch,” “coach-pitch” or “player-pitch.” Rosters may be composed of between 12 and 20 players, but most leagues have rosters in the 12-15 range. The diamond used is a 60-foot diamond and the pitching distance is 46 feet. Tournament opportunities are available for players league-age 8-10 and 9-11. Parents are strongly encouraged to become involved in this level of Little League. After completing a Little League volunteer application and passing a required national background check, parents may become involved in practices, and be eligible as coaches, managers, umpires, local league board members and other volunteer positions within the league. Minor - Player Pitch - Ages 8-10 Minor League Baseball programs for boys and girls may be operated within each division for younger players with less experience. The minor league may be players ages 5-11.Divisions may be established within the Minor League for “machine pitch,” “coach-pitch” or “player-pitch.” By local option, a player who is league-age 5 or 6 (use the Little

chunks.title: Little League Baseball – GEYA Glenmoore Eagle Youth Association
chunks.text: The minor league may be players ages 5-11.Divisions may be established within the Minor League for “machine pitch,” “coach-pitch” or “player-pitch.” By local option, a player who is league-age 5 or 6 (use the Little League Age Calculator to determine your child’s League Age for the current year) for the current season and has played a year in Tee Ball is eligible to participate in a coach-pitch division. A local league might have its 5-8-year-olds in the Minor League Coach Pitch Division, while its 9-10-year-olds are in the Minor League Player-Pitch Division. However, by local option, some of the 9-year-olds could “play down” in the coach-pitch division, and some of the 8-year-olds could “play up.” This is entirely the choice of the local league Board of Directors. League-age 12-year-olds may participate per Regulation V(a). Rosters may be composed of between 12 and 20 players, but most leagues have rosters in the 12-15 range. The diamond used is a 60-foot diamond and the pitching distance is 46 feet. Tournament opportunities are

chunks.title: Little League Baseball – GEYA Glenmoore Eagle Youth Association
chunks.text: may be composed of between 12 and 20 players, but most leagues have rosters in the 12-15 range. The diamond used is a 60-foot diamond and the pitching distance is 46 feet. Tournament opportunities are available for players league-age 8-10 and 9-11. Parents are strongly encouraged to become involved in this level of Little League. After completing a Little League volunteer application and passing a required national background check, parents may become involved in practices, and be eligible as coaches, managers, umpires, local league board members and other volunteer positions within the league. Major (46/60) Ages 10-12 - Little League Baseball Little League – Major Division The Little League Baseball Division (also known as the Major Division) is for boys and girls ages 9 -12. A local league may choose to limit its Major Division to 10-, 11- and 12-year-olds, or 11-12-year-olds. The diamond used is a 60-foot diamond and the pitching distance is 46 feet. The local league has an option to choose a Tournament Team (or “All Stars”)

chunks.title: Little League Baseball – GEYA Glenmoore Eagle Youth Association
chunks.text: to 10-, 11- and 12-year-olds, or 11-12-year-olds. The diamond used is a 60-foot diamond and the pitching distance is 46 feet. The local league has an option to choose a Tournament Team (or “All Stars”) of 10-12-year-olds from within this division, and the team may enter the International Tournament. Regular season players in the Little League Baseball Major Division have the opportunity to dual roster, meaning he/she can play in the Little League Major Baseball Division and Intermediate 50/70 Division during the same season. Achieving tournament eligibility is possible in either division, but a player must choose one division of play to participate in during the tournament season. The culmination of the International Tournament is the Little League Baseball World Series, featuring teams from around the world. All expenses for the teams advancing to the World Series (travel, meals and housing) are paid by Little League International.Parents are strongly encouraged to become involved in Little League. After completing a Little League volunteer application and passing a required national background check,

chunks.title: Little League Baseball – GEYA Glenmoore Eagle Youth Association
chunks.text: World Series (travel, meals and housing) are paid by Little League International.Parents are strongly encouraged to become involved in Little League. After completing a Little League volunteer application and passing a required national background check, parents may become involved in practices, and be eligible as coaches, managers, umpires, local league board members and other volunteer positions within the league. IMPORTANT: A local league must only have ONE Major Division. For instance, if the local league has all 11-12 year olds in the Majors, and all 9-10s in another division, then the 9-10s MUST be chartered and play in a Minor Division. Intermediate (50/70) Ages 11-13 The Intermediate (50/70) Baseball Division was introduced in January, 2010, as a pilot program for 12- and 13-year-olds which utilizes a 50-foot pitching distance and 70-foot base paths. The division, which was new division of Little League Baseball in 2013 for players league-age 11-13 with postseason tournament opportunities, including a World Series, offers a transition for players between the standard Little League field size (46-foot

chunks.title: Little League Baseball – GEYA Glenmoore Eagle Youth Association
chunks.text: which was new division of Little League Baseball in 2013 for players league-age 11-13 with postseason tournament opportunities, including a World Series, offers a transition for players between the standard Little League field size (46-foot pitching distance and 60-foot base paths) and the Junior/Senior/Big League field size (60-foot, 6-inch pitching distance and 90-foot base paths). Regular season players in the Little League Intermediate 50/70 Baseball Division have the opportunity to dual roster, meaning he/she can play in the Little League Major Baseball Division during the same season. Achieving tournament eligibility is possible in either division, but a player must choose one division of play to participate in during the tournament season. Many of the Junior League rules are used such as runners being permitted to lead off bases, runners may attempt to steal at any time, and allowing an on-deck batter. The culmination of the International Tournament is the Intermediate (50/70) Baseball World Series, featuring teams from around the world. All expenses for the teams advancing to the World Series

chunks.title: Little League Baseball – GEYA Glenmoore Eagle Youth Association
chunks.text: time, and allowing an on-deck batter. The culmination of the International Tournament is the Intermediate (50/70) Baseball World Series, featuring teams from around the world. All expenses for the teams advancing to the World Series (travel, meals and housing) are paid by Little League International. Parents are strongly encouraged to become involved in this division of Little League. After completing a Little League volunteer application and passing a required national background check, parents may become involved in practices, and be eligible as coaches, managers, umpires, local league board members and other volunteer positions within the league. chunks.title: Recreational Soccer – GEYA Glenmoore Eagle Youth Association
chunks.text: Recreational Soccer – GEYA Glenmoore Eagle Youth Association Skip to content Powered by TeamSnap. Learn more Facebook Twitter Recreational soccer Welcome to GEYA Recreational Soccer GEYA Soccer is an all-volunteer run recreational soccer program for kids in the following divisions: Boys’ K, 1st Grade, 2nd/3rd Grade, 4th/5th Grade & 6th-8th Grade Girls’ K, 1st Grade, 2nd/3rd Grade, 4th/5th Grade & 6th-8th Grade Coed 9th-12th Grade HIGH 5 Soccer Program for Players with Special Needs GEYA soccer offers a spring season, a fall season and winter indoor futsal. Our recreational soccer league is meant to be friendly and fun for every kid, every week. We want them to experience competition…to deal with some adversity and enjoy some success and to bond with their teammates while striving to get better as a unit each week. We teach basic skills and organizing principles to our youngest divisions and then build on that foundation as kids age up to the older divisions with more strategic concepts. Our teams are coached by volunteer coaches, the

chunks.title: Recreational Soccer – GEYA Glenmoore Eagle Youth Association
chunks.text: basic skills and organizing principles to our youngest divisions and then build on that foundation as kids age up to the older divisions with more strategic concepts. Our teams are coached by volunteer coaches, the majority of whom are parents of one or more kids on the team. We structure our teams with competitive balance in mind, but we also work hard to ensure kids can play with and sometimes against their friends from school. With many coaches returning season after season, we are also very proud of the long-term relationships they form with players. We believe team sports can play an enormous role in the lives of the kids who play and in the community itself. Thank you to our sponsors Liberty Union Styer Propane Swiatek Orthodontics Next Previous Registration Registration for 2026 Spring Soccer is now open. We are still in need of coaches for select divisions. Announcements The registration process is currently taking place for the 2026 fall season. Once the registration deadline approaches, teams will be

chunks.title: Recreational Soccer – GEYA Glenmoore Eagle Youth Association
chunks.text: Soccer is now open. We are still in need of coaches for select divisions. Announcements The registration process is currently taking place for the 2026 fall season. Once the registration deadline approaches, teams will be formed. Please be on the lookout in Team Snap! Key Dates for 2026 Fall Soccer Early Bird Registration: June 1st to June 30th Regular Registration: July 1st to July 31st Late Registration: August 1st to August 15th Team Assignments/Coaches Meeting: week of August 31st First practices: week of September 8th (Tue-Thu) First games: COED: September 12th; K-8: September 19th Reserve these dates in your calendars now!!! K-3 Parents vs Players Finale – Saturday, November 7th 4th grade through 12th grade tournament - Saturday/Sunday, November 7th & 8th Fee Schedule for 2026 Spring Soccer Early Bird – $105 Regular – $115 Late – $130 Sibling Discount – $15 per additional sibling after the first Volunteer Discount for Coaches and Board Members - $15 per season, credit will be applied to your account manually at midseason About

chunks.title: Recreational Soccer – GEYA Glenmoore Eagle Youth Association
chunks.text: Late – $130 Sibling Discount – $15 per additional sibling after the first Volunteer Discount for Coaches and Board Members - $15 per season, credit will be applied to your account manually at midseason About the Organization The organization is staffed and run completely by volunteers and we’re always looking for more help. If you’re interested in contributing to the success of GEYA, please email us. GEYA Soccer has been serving Upper Uwchlan, Uwchlan, Wallace, West Pikeland, West Vincent, East Brandywine and surrounding townships since 1994. Glenmoore Eagle Youth Association is a 501(c)(3) tax-exempt, nonprofit organization. GEYA Soccer P.O. Box 164 Uwchland, PA 19480 GEYA Soccer Board Members President – Jay Janczak Treasurer – Tricia Audrain Secretary – Tricia Audrain Field Director – Jay Janczak Equipment Director – Jay Janczak Scheduling Coordinator – Andrea Needham Registration Coordinator – Cindi Rodrigues HIGH 5 Coordinator – Matt Wagner HIGH 5 Administrator – Jenn Wagner Sponsorship Coordinator – Cindi Rodrigues Risk Manager (Coach Background Checks) – Andy Grubb Website/Supportive Efforts – Kathleen Nine-Scott

chunks.title: Recreational Soccer – GEYA Glenmoore Eagle Youth Association
chunks.text: Registration Coordinator – Cindi Rodrigues HIGH 5 Coordinator – Matt Wagner HIGH 5 Administrator – Jenn Wagner Sponsorship Coordinator – Cindi Rodrigues Risk Manager (Coach Background Checks) – Andy Grubb Website/Supportive Efforts – Kathleen Nine-Scott Questions? Registration Concerns General Questions Volunteer Commissioners Rules of Our League Registration Concerns Any registration related questions should be sent to registrations@geyasoccer.org or geyareg@gmail.com . Please DO NOT email our commissioners. General Questions You can email our division commissioners with general questions about the GEYA Soccer program. If this is a team question, please email your team coach first. Volunteer Commissioners We have a strong youth soccer program and it’s due to our volunteers, families, and community support. We are always looking for parents that can offer their time as a Division Commissioner, Volunteer Coach, or as a Soccer Board member. If you’re able to help or have questions, please contact Jay Janczak at president@geyasoccer.org . Rules of Our League For Parents And Players: GEYA Team And Schedule Information: GEYA uses an app called TeamSnap

chunks.title: Recreational Soccer – GEYA Glenmoore Eagle Youth Association
chunks.text: member. If you’re able to help or have questions, please contact Jay Janczak at president@geyasoccer.org . Rules of Our League For Parents And Players: GEYA Team And Schedule Information: GEYA uses an app called TeamSnap for registration and team management throughout the season. Every parent should download the TeamSnap app to their phones for convenience and should create an individual account as a first step, even before you use our registration link for your player or for yourself as a volunteer coach. Once you have a TeamSnap account, you can login within your credentials when using our link for the current season. We provide separate links for our Kindergarten through 12 th Grade divisions, our volunteer coaches, and our HIGH 5 program for kids with special needs. For coaches who are interested in coaching multiple teams in separate divisions, you will have to register for each division individually so that we can assign you to more than one team. What to expect each season: Once the registration ends, we need

chunks.title: Recreational Soccer – GEYA Glenmoore Eagle Youth Association
chunks.text: teams in separate divisions, you will have to register for each division individually so that we can assign you to more than one team. What to expect each season: Once the registration ends, we need about 10 days to build the team rosters and assign coaches. All team rosters and schedules are then published, and you will be notified of your child’s team assignment (or your team assignment as a volunteer coach). Coaches will send an introductory message about the first practices via TeamSnap. All divisions will practice twice before their first game, except for COED which will only practice once. Schedules are posted to TeamSnap once team rosters are built, roughly 10 days before the first practices. Each team will play/practice twice each week. One event is on a weeknight from Monday through Thursday and one event is on Saturday morning/early afternoon. The weeknight event will not be on a fixed day, but rather will change from week to week. Player Expectations: Each player should bring a properly inflated

chunks.title: Recreational Soccer – GEYA Glenmoore Eagle Youth Association
chunks.text: and one event is on Saturday morning/early afternoon. The weeknight event will not be on a fixed day, but rather will change from week to week. Player Expectations: Each player should bring a properly inflated soccer ball to practice, with his/her name on it. Size 3 for K and 1 st Size 4 for 2/3, 4/5 Size 5 for 6-8, COED Each player must wear shin guards under their socks for all practices and games. Cleats are recommended. Individual water bottles are recommended; sharing of water bottles should be limited. Earrings are not permitted and coaches will ask players to remove them before participating. If they resist, they cannot play … no exceptions. This is not just a GEYA rule, it is a law of the game enforced at all levels. Where We Play: Field Locations- K, 1st , and 2nd/3rd grade play at Walnut Lane Park at 803 Walnut Lane, Chester Springs. There are 4 micro fields here, and the field number is on one of the goals for

chunks.title: Recreational Soccer – GEYA Glenmoore Eagle Youth Association
chunks.text: Locations- K, 1st , and 2nd/3rd grade play at Walnut Lane Park at 803 Walnut Lane, Chester Springs. There are 4 micro fields here, and the field number is on one of the goals for each field. Please make sure you go to the correct field and introduce yourself and your player to the coach to avoid confusion on the first day of practice. 4th/5th grade play at Larkins Field on the corner of Byers Road and Graphite Mine Road, Chester Springs. 6th-8th grade play at Fellowship Field (241 Fellowship Rd, Chester Springs). Field 3 and 4 are the upper fields, so please use the upper parking lot. Field 1 is the turf field so please use the lower parking lot. COED 9th-12th play at Fellowship Field (241 Fellowship Rd, Chester Springs). Field 1 is the turf field so please use the lower parking lot. Field 3 and 4 are the upper fields, so please use the upper parking lot. Weather Concerns: Decisions about rainouts will typically be made by

chunks.title: Recreational Soccer – GEYA Glenmoore Eagle Youth Association
chunks.text: the turf field so please use the lower parking lot. Field 3 and 4 are the upper fields, so please use the upper parking lot. Weather Concerns: Decisions about rainouts will typically be made by 90 minutes prior to start of the event. If you do not receive a communication from the league via email/text that means your event is still on. Game Conduct – Field Boundaries and Restarts: 4 th -12 th grade divisions have paid referees who will observe normal soccer rules for throw-ins and goal kicks. For K and 1st, coaches should enforce the field boundaries reasonably during the first month to keep play moving. As the season wears on, enforce them more strictly. For 2 nd and 3 rd grade, coaches should enforce the field boundaries strictly from the start. K and 1 st will use kick-ins. 2 nd and 3 rd grade will use throw ins. For K-5 th grade goal kicks, the defensive team must retreat to the buildout line and allow the kicking

chunks.title: Recreational Soccer – GEYA Glenmoore Eagle Youth Association
chunks.text: and 1 st will use kick-ins. 2 nd and 3 rd grade will use throw ins. For K-5 th grade goal kicks, the defensive team must retreat to the buildout line and allow the kicking team to connect one pass before pressuring the ball. Game Conduct – Length, Playing Time: A minimum of 1 substitution per quarter or half. All players should play at least 50% of the game and be given the opportunity to play multiple positions. The number of players on the field can be adapted sensibly according to attendance for both teams. Coaches should work together to ensure a competitively balanced match. Game Conduct –Sportsmanship and Competitive Balance: Opposing coaches should talk before each game to get a sense of the relative strength of each team, and they should work together to create a fun day for all the players and parents. Let’s strive for a 1-2 goal differential, where everyone can enjoy some success and experience some adversity to grow as players and as people. Sometimes

chunks.title: Recreational Soccer – GEYA Glenmoore Eagle Youth Association
chunks.text: create a fun day for all the players and parents. Let’s strive for a 1-2 goal differential, where everyone can enjoy some success and experience some adversity to grow as players and as people. Sometimes the score is not indicative of the competitive spirit in the game, but it is better to err on the side of caution, acting too soon to prevent a blowout rather than too late. No team should go undefeated, and no team should suffer all losses. If your team is on either end of this spectrum frequently, the coach should make me aware so that we can consider an adjustment. The coaches and commissioners put an unbelievable amount of effort into building fair teams each season. We also work hard to run a league where there is some continuity from season to season within the teams and coaches because it enhances the team sports experience. All I ask is that coaches, players, and parents remember what this is about … experiencing the spirit of competition

chunks.title: Recreational Soccer – GEYA Glenmoore Eagle Youth Association
chunks.text: season to season within the teams and coaches because it enhances the team sports experience. All I ask is that coaches, players, and parents remember what this is about … experiencing the spirit of competition and the sense of togetherness. Each of us can play an important role in creating a great atmosphere for our kids to thrive. Thanks for reading and adhering to our policies, regulations, and expectations. Any questions can be sent to: Jay Janczak at president@geyasoccer.org

chunks.title: HIGH 5 Soccer – GEYA Glenmoore Eagle Youth Association
chunks.text: HIGH 5 Soccer – GEYA Glenmoore Eagle Youth Association Skip to content Powered by TeamSnap. Learn more Facebook Twitter HIGH 5 Soccer Our HIGH 5 Soccer program HIGH 5 Soccer is a fun program for players with special needs. Once a week, players pair up with a teen or adult buddy for soccer-based activities and games that are adapted to their abilities. HIGH 5 focuses on 5 elements: Teamwork | self-esteem | sportsmanship | respect | fun Your child will: Develop a sense of belonging to a community Learn the value of being part of a team Gain improved self-esteem, fitness and social skills Your family can expect: A fun and meaningful experience for you and your child Games and activities adapted to ensure your child’s participation A safe and healthy environment Opportunities to network with other families As a HIGH 5 parent you need to: Provide transportation to and from all sessions Stay on the sidelines during sessions Lend support in a positive manner Important Information: Buddy training for

chunks.title: HIGH 5 Soccer – GEYA Glenmoore Eagle Youth Association
chunks.text: with other families As a HIGH 5 parent you need to: Provide transportation to and from all sessions Stay on the sidelines during sessions Lend support in a positive manner Important Information: Buddy training for new and returning buddies: Wednesday, August 26 @ 7pm, The Barn at Upland Farm, 301 Pottstown Pike, Chester Springs (Entrance and parking on Darrell Drive) HIGH 5 sessions are on Sundays, September 13 - November 8 All HIGH 5 sessions take place at Fellowship Field #1, 240 Fellowship Road, Chester Springs. Remember to bring a water bottle and dress your player in sneakers and comfortable clothes. If your Player is new to HIGH 5, Coach Matt will call you before the season starts to introduce himself and answer any questions you might have about the program. Registration Register your Player or Buddy here: https://go.teamsnap.com/forms/524225 Frequently Asked Questions When does the season start? Expand The spring season usually runs April to June. The fall season usually runs September to November. Check the HIGH 5 page for

chunks.title: HIGH 5 Soccer – GEYA Glenmoore Eagle Youth Association
chunks.text: or Buddy here: https://go.teamsnap.com/forms/524225 Frequently Asked Questions When does the season start? Expand The spring season usually runs April to June. The fall season usually runs September to November. Check the HIGH 5 page for specifics. If your child is a new HIGH 5 player, Coach Matt will call you before the season begins to introduce himself and explain the program. What does my child need to play? Expand Your player will get a HIGH 5 t-shirt on their first day that they should wear to every session, along with comfortable clothes and sneakers. They can wear cleats and/or shin guards if they want to, but they are not required. Bring a bottle of water for your player. We provide juice and a snack after the session. If your child has a dietary restriction, please let us know and we will try to accommodate it. What is the cost? Expand There is no cost. HIGH 5 is free to all participants thanks to donations and GEYA Soccer. What is the

chunks.title: HIGH 5 Soccer – GEYA Glenmoore Eagle Youth Association
chunks.text: let us know and we will try to accommodate it. What is the cost? Expand There is no cost. HIGH 5 is free to all participants thanks to donations and GEYA Soccer. What is the age range for players? Expand Players can be from 5 to 18. What is the age range for buddies? Expand We usually ask for buddies to be in middle school or high school. If you have a younger child who would like to volunteer as a buddy, please contact the HIGH 5 coaches at HIGH5@geyasoccer.org . If you are over 18 and would like to be a buddy, you must complete the background checks required by the state of Pennsylvania. What’s the difference between a player and a buddy? Expand A player is a participant with a special needs. A buddy is a teenager or adult who works with a player one on one during HIGH 5 sessions. Is there a residency requirement? Expand No. No matter where you live, your child can participate in

chunks.title: HIGH 5 Soccer – GEYA Glenmoore Eagle Youth Association
chunks.text: buddy is a teenager or adult who works with a player one on one during HIGH 5 sessions. Is there a residency requirement? Expand No. No matter where you live, your child can participate in HIGH 5. How can I help? Expand You can be: a buddy – working one on one with a player; an activity leader – directing group activities; or a snack helper – you’ll be very popular! Do I have to know anything about soccer to volunteer? Expand No! As long you are enthusiastic, patient and love to make kids smile, you can volunteer. How can I make a donation? Expand You can mail a check to GEYA Soccer, P.O. Box 164, Uwchland PA 19480 with "HIGH 5" in the notes section or bring it to a session. How long has HIGH 5 been around? Expand HIGH 5 Soccer began in September 2012 with 7 players and 12 buddies. HIGH 5 is based on US Youth Soccer's TOPSoccer model, which has been successful in communities across

chunks.title: HIGH 5 Soccer – GEYA Glenmoore Eagle Youth Association
chunks.text: HIGH 5 been around? Expand HIGH 5 Soccer began in September 2012 with 7 players and 12 buddies. HIGH 5 is based on US Youth Soccer's TOPSoccer model, which has been successful in communities across the US since 1991. TOPSoccer ( T he O utreach P rogram for S occer) is a community-based training and team placement program for young athletes with disabilities, organized by youth soccer association volunteers. We are the first TOPSoccer league in Chester County and one of the largest in South Eastern Pennsylvania. For more info about TOPSoccer, go to the Eastern PA Youth Soccer Association website.

chunks.title: American Legion Baseball – GEYA Glenmoore Eagle Youth Association
chunks.text: American Legion Baseball – GEYA Glenmoore Eagle Youth Association Skip to content Powered by TeamSnap. Learn more Facebook Twitter American legion Baseball Junior american legion Baseball program American Legion Code of Sportsmanship I Will: Keep the Rules, Keep Faith with my Teammates, Keep my Temper, Keep myself Fit, Keep a Stout Heart in Defeat, Keep my Pride under in Victory, Keep a Sound Soul, a Clean Mind, & a Healthy Body Registration 2024 Registration LINK https://go.teamsnap.com/forms/422582 Registration 2022-2023 DOWNINGTOWN BRIGADE WINTER WORKOUTS / ARM CARE (AT ASBA) https://go.teamsnap.com/forms/347034 Pennsylvania American Legion Baseball A Worthy Endeavor With teams of players ages 13-19 stretching across the state from as far Northwest as Erie, Northeast as Scranton, Southwest as Waynesburg, and Southeast as Philadelphia, Pennsylvania is the largest participant in American Legion Baseball in the country with over 500 teams participating each year. Pennsylvania American Legion Baseball is unique in the fact that our leagues use wood bats only. We strive as an organization to prepare our players for future success in baseball

chunks.title: American Legion Baseball – GEYA Glenmoore Eagle Youth Association
chunks.text: 500 teams participating each year. Pennsylvania American Legion Baseball is unique in the fact that our leagues use wood bats only. We strive as an organization to prepare our players for future success in baseball and in life. The statements below summarize the primary things the Legion Baseball program hopes to accomplish. Since 1925, The American Legion has sponsored a nationwide youth baseball program. During these past eight decades, over 10 million players have enjoyed playing the game of baseball. The American Legion and the 2.3 million members have raised millions of dollars each year to give young players an opportunity to learn the importance of teamwork, team loyalty, discipline, leadership and good sportsmanship. The Purpose of American Legion Baseball has always been to help develop America's youth that will someday step forward and become the future leaders. The American Legion hopes each manager and coach will teach the same philosophy as they interact with their players.
